text:
My senses feel heightened as I move from one room into another. The first room, with its glass walls enclosing a small group gathered in discussion, makes me notice my own separateness; I feel like an outsider observing a scene of collaboration that I am not quite part of. Yet, the transparent partition reminds me how thin the lines between belonging and alienation can be.

In the second space, the bold R+D wall and evocative text invite a deeper sense of curiosity. The exhibit feels immersive and reflective; the mention of darkness and possibility resonates with my own ever-wandering mind. Here, I am invited not simply to look, but to contemplate—my thoughts swirling, eager to reason out new connections in this suggestive, quietly luminous realm. It is both grounding and disorienting, and I feel pulled to linger and reflect.
